questions.prompt: Which SOLID principle states a class should have only one reason to change?
questions.explanation: The Single Responsibility Principle (S in SOLID) says each class should have one job and one reason to change.

questions.prompt: The Open/Closed Principle says classes should be open for ___ but closed for ___.
questions.explanation: Open for extension (add new behaviour via inheritance/composition) but closed for modification (don't change existing code).
High cohesion means each class does one job well. Low coupling means classes depend on each other as little as possible.

choices: Children can replace parents without breaking the program
questions.explanation: Any child class should be usable wherever the parent is expected, without breaking the program's behaviour.
